commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=79f2bed2b4e7b4f5486b397a4cb97131ae21196d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Android realtime Talk frame/audio handling, Android/Web transcript accumulation and rendering, and gateway realtime relay consult routing with focused tests and changelog entries.

Reproducibility: Partially yes: source inspection shows current main sends finalized relay transcripts directly back to the realtime provider, while the PR forces an OpenClaw consult fallback; the supplied screenshots also reproduce visible transcript/layout issues. Audio playback stability and the relay consult result path still lack inspectable real-environment proof.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer decision on forced-consult routing policy plus contributor-supplied real playback/relay proof; this is not a safe automated repair-only path.

target.title: Stabilize realtime Talk turns, playback, and consults
target.body:
## Summary

- Serialize Android Gateway websocket frame handling and queue realtime PCM playback so assistant audio and transcript events are applied in order.
- Accumulate realtime transcript chunks and split alternating user/assistant turns correctly in Android and Web.
- Move Web realtime Talk bubbles into the main chat thread so voice turns flow downward like normal chat instead of living inside the composer.
- Force/route Web realtime consults through OpenClaw when the provider skips the consult, then keep Web gateway-relay answers behind the OpenClaw result instead of re-prompting Google with the raw user request.
- Finalize forced Web consult relay results correctly for browser `{ result }` payloads and native-consult overlap cases.

## Root Cause

Android Gateway websocket frames were dispatched on separate coroutines, so realtime transcript/audio events could race. Android transcript deltas were also treated like full snapshots, which could leave only the latest assistant fragment visible, and PCM chunks were written directly from event handling with partial-write/choppy-playback risk.

The shared realtime conversation accumulator only closed the active user turn when an assistant turn began. It did not consistently close the active assistant turn when a genuinely new user turn began, so alternating turns could collapse into prior bubbles.

Web additionally rendered realtime Talk turns inside the composer/input box with a capped mini-scroll area. Even when the accumulator had separate entries, the layout did not behave like the main chat transcript.

For Web realtime consults, the provider can produce a final transcript without invoking `openclaw_agent_consult`. The relay now forces the consult in that case. The later live false-success report showed two consult tool calls, no final `tool.result`, then `session.closed`; matching session history showed the OpenClaw consult had started but had not produced a successful final action result. Code inspection showed the Web gateway relay also sent the recognized user sentence back into the realtime provider as a normal user message while the separate OpenClaw consult path was pending. That allowed the provider to speak from stale/provider context even when OpenClaw had not completed the tool-backed action.

The final review pass also found that browser forced-consult results use `{ result: "..." }`, while the relay only treated `{ text: "..." }` as speakable text, and that matching native consult continuations needed to be cleared before asking the realtime bridge to speak the final forced-consult answer.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Android realtime transcript/audio stability, Android/Web alternating Talk bubble separation, Web Talk bubble layout flow, Web realtime forced consult routing, Web realtime provider false-success speech while OpenClaw consult is still pending or aborted, and forced Web consult final speech after browser/native result completion.

Real environment tested: Focused Android unit-test/ktlint environment, focused Web/Node Vitest environment, and live production update path on the operator gateway with PR #84181 overlaid. Live session history and relay logs from May 19, 2026 were inspected with personal identifiers and private task details omitted from the PR text.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.voice.TalkModeManagerTest`; `./gradlew :app:ktlintCheck`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/realtime-talk-conversation.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app.talk.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle-connect.node.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/styles/chat/layout.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/talk-realtime-relay.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts src/gateway/talk-realtime-relay.test.ts`; `pnpm tsgo:core`; targeted `pnpm format:check`; `git diff --check`; `~/.codex/skills/codex-review/scripts/codex-review --mode local`; `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_IGNORE_PRS=84219 oc-update --skip-codex`.

Evidence after fix: Android focused unit and ktlint checks passed. Web realtime conversation/view/lifecycle tests passed: 4 files, 51 tests. Web chat layout styles passed: 1 file, 8 tests. Talk consult/gateway tests passed: 3 files, 43 tests. Gateway realtime relay tests passed: 3 files, 27 tests. After reverting the exact-context guardrail commit, the combined focused talk suite passed: 6 files, 70 tests. After the final forced-consult relay fix and rebase, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/talk-realtime-relay.test.ts` passed again: 3 files, 27 tests; `pnpm tsgo:core`, targeted `pnpm format:check`, and `git diff --check` passed. Codex review reported no accepted/actionable findings. `oc-update --skip-codex` with PR #84219 excluded applied PR #84181, built core, built UI, linked the CLI, reinstalled/restarted the daemon, and completed health/security audit.

Observed result after fix: Android now keeps realtime events ordered, accumulates transcript chunks, closes old turns when the next speaker starts, and writes playback through a queued AudioTrack writer. Web now renders realtime Talk turns in the main chat thread, keeps alternating turns separate, auto-scrolls on realtime transcript updates, forces an OpenClaw consult when the realtime provider skips one, and no longer sends the raw recognized request back to the realtime provider before the OpenClaw consult result is ready. The provider only receives the existing checking/final prompts around the OpenClaw result path. After the final head was applied through `oc-update`, the operator confirmed the live production runtime is running without issue.

What was not tested: No fresh hardware recording or screenshot artifact is attached to this PR. I did not run a full Android device realtime Talk smoke after the final Web-only relay-finalization commit. The `oc-update` health check completed but reported transient gateway event-loop degradation from CPU/event-loop delay; the security audit reported pre-existing local/config warnings unrelated to this PR.

## Verification

- PASS: `./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.voice.TalkModeManagerTest`
- PASS: `./gradlew :app:ktlintCheck`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/realtime-talk-conversation.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app.talk.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle-connect.node.test.ts`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/styles/chat/layout.test.ts`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/talk-realtime-relay.test.ts`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts src/gateway/talk-realtime-relay.test.ts`
- PASS: `pnpm tsgo:core`
- PASS: targeted `pnpm format:check`
- PASS: `git diff --check`
- PASS: `~/.codex/skills/codex-review/scripts/codex-review --mode local`
- PASS: `OPENCLAW_UPDATE_IGNORE_PRS=84219 oc-update --skip-codex`
- LIVE: PR #84181 is running in production through the update overlay without reported issue.
- NOT RUN: full Android app/device realtime Talk smoke after the final Web-only relay-finalization commit.
